claim_boundary: This work contract governs only the declared target skill and local run evidence. It does not prove packaged CLI support, package publication, external services, future AI correctness, or broader release readiness.

closure_rules.scope: declared run scope only; accepted closure is intentionally not allowed

forbidden_shortcuts.summary: Do not move directly from intake or planning to closure without current phase evidence and required checks.

integration_claim_boundary: No native route/check system is declared for this generated target, so SkillGuard owns this runtime contract until a native system is added. If a native route/check system appears later, regenerate or edit this contract as native-integrated or hybrid-extension; duplicate SkillGuard-owned execution paths are invalid.

native_check_inventory.summary: description: "Use when maintaining, reviewing, or releasing Codex skill repositories and skill-like workflows that need inventory, activation-boundary checks, hard validation gates, and evi.
Do not cite or require scripts, fixtures, examples, tests, package commands, releases, git remotes, or publication records unless they exist in the current filesystem and were inspected for.
Frontmatter: the file starts with closed YAML frontmatter containing name: skillguard and a specific description.
Gates: Hard Gates lists the checks that must stay visible and cannot be replaced by confidence, intent, or stale reports.
SkillGuard is a guardrail workflow for Codex skill maintenance and skill-release work. It helps agents inspect skill materials, clarify activation boundaries, enforce hard validation gates,.
Start from the current layout instead of assuming generated files exist. SkillGuard can run from the source repository layout or from the installed Codex skill layout.
This SKILL.md is the activation and routing entrypoint for the SkillGuard skill. It is not evidence that every referenced workflow, script, fixture, test, package command, release artifact,.
Treat local scripts/ and fixtures/ under this skill as evidence only after direct inspection in the current task finds those paths and their current content. Treat repository tests and exam.
Use assets/schemas/ and assets/templates/ for local schema and template checks.
Use other .skillguard/ material under the skill directory when the task asks for maintained SkillGuard records, evidence, reports, or self-check material.
Use .skillguard/checks/ for local runtime check script stubs and .skillguard/runs/ for run records created before non-trivial skill work begins.
Use SkillGuard to keep public skill repositories honest about what exists, what was checked, what still needs review, and what must be blocked before release or adoption.
Use .skillguard/work-contract.json and .skillguard/check_manifest.json when a task needs runtime contract routing, phase gates, or closure rules.
Use the skillguard-global-router skill and the scan-global-skills, build-global-registry, check-global-registry, resolve-global-skill, render-global-prompt, install-global-prompt, check-glo.
Use this map when checking whether the entrypoint itself is acceptable
Workflow: Required Workflow orders inspection, target confirmation, inventory, deterministic checks, judgment checks, evidence collection, and pass/fail/block reporting.

not_parallel_route_proof.evidence_source: SKILL.md and adjacent target materials inspected during contract compilation
not_parallel_route_proof.summary: This runtime-owner contract is valid only when no native route/check owner is present.

phases.summary: Confirm target, requested outcome, route, and claim boundary.
Inspect current target files and supported local materials.
Collect current direct evidence for the declared scope.
Run required checks and quality-floor gates.
Close only inside the evidence-backed claim boundary.

quality_floors.summary: Do not replace required command or file evidence with prose-only claims.

route_inventory.summary: Target workflow or route section: Deep Contract Mode
Target workflow or route section: Do Not Use When
Entrypoint described by frontmatter: Use when maintaining, reviewing, or releasing Codex skill repositories and skill-like workflows that need inventory, activation-boundary checks, hard va.
Target workflow or route section: Entrypoint Acceptance Map
Target workflow or route section: Entrypoint Scope
Target workflow or route section: Global Router Mode
Target workflow or route section: Required Workflow
Target workflow or route section: Runtime Contract Mode
Target workflow or route section: Use When

do_not_use_when: The task is outside this skill's activation boundary.
routes.summary: Inspect the target skill and close only after required checks pass.

runtime_lock_policy.applies_when: Before and during non-trivial work that uses this covered target skill.
runtime_lock_policy.summary: The covered skill keeps its own entrypoint and must expose route, stage, check, evidence, and closure blockers from this target-local contract.

source_requirements.summary: Deep contract work must extract target-specific rules and map them to checks, evidence, and closure blockers.
Global router work must enforce registry and managed prompt freshness before routing claims.
Native and hybrid contracts must bind the target skill's native route/check owner and must not create a parallel execution route.
Runtime contract work must enforce selected route, run record, phase evidence, checks, quality floors, and closure gates.
Covered target skills must expose a target-local runtime lock before work starts and before closure.
Check README-facing release gates such as bilingual mirror coverage, text-to-image hero provenance, README model evidence, version consistency, command-surface wording, and public-boundary.
Check whether a skill's description is too broad, too vague, or likely to activate for unrelated tasks.
Compare parent status against child evidence so a suite or release summary does not hide stale, failed, missing, or skipped checks.
Create, update, audit, or prepare a Codex skill or skill-like workflow.
description: "Use when maintaining, reviewing, or releasing Codex skill repositories and skill-like workflows that need inventory, activation-boundary checks, hard validation gates, and evi.
Frontmatter: the file starts with closed YAML frontmatter containing name: skillguard and a specific description.
Gates: Hard Gates lists the checks that must stay visible and cannot be replaced by confidence, intent, or stale reports.
If a current check cannot support one of these criteria, report the specific gap. Do not compensate by adding unrelated files, broad repository edits, generated outputs, git operations, pac.
In the source repository layout, treat this file as the Codex skill entrypoint under .agents/skills/skillguard/SKILL.md; in the installed layout, treat local SKILL.md as the entrypoint.
Local routing: Local Material Routing identifies the current repository materials and separates existing local paths from absent optional paths.
Maintenance: SkillGuard Maintenance says when this entrypoint and related public files must be kept in sync.
Output: Output Requirements defines the evidence, blocker, skipped-check, residual-risk, and claim-boundary fields expected in SkillGuard reports.
Produce a status report that needs current evidence, blockers, skipped checks, residual risk, and claim boundaries.
Review a skill repository for public readiness, activation clarity, privacy safety, metadata consistency, or release claims.
SkillGuard is a guardrail workflow for Codex skill maintenance and skill-release work. It helps agents inspect skill materials, clarify activation boundaries, enforce hard validation gates,.
This SKILL.md is the activation and routing entrypoint for the SkillGuard skill. It is not evidence that every referenced workflow, script, fixture, test, package command, release artifact,.
Use this map when checking whether the entrypoint itself is acceptable
The target section 'Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Validate a maintained skill target, suite summary, README, SKILL.md, metadata, fixtures, schemas, scripts, or check evidence.
When a task asks for implementation, validation, packaging, publication, or release work, inspect the current filesystem first and report missing local materials as absent or skipped. Do no.
Workflow: Required Workflow orders inspection, target confirmation, inventory, deterministic checks, judgment checks, evidence collection, and pass/fail/block reporting.
Do not cite or require scripts, fixtures, examples, tests, package commands, releases, git remotes, or publication records unless they exist in the current filesystem and were inspected for.
Use assets/schemas/ and assets/templates/ for local schema and template checks.

Use SkillGuard to keep public skill repositories honest about what exists, what was checked, what still needs review, and what must be blocked before release or adoption.
Use .skillguard/work-contract.json and .skillguard/check_manifest.json when a task needs runtime contract routing, phase gates, or closure rules.
The target skill description must be represented in the contract: Use when maintaining, reviewing, or releasing Codex skill repositories and skill-like workflows that need inventory, activa.
The target skill entrypoint and hard gates must be represented by the runtime contract.
Declared hard gates and required workflow steps must be represented by checks and closure blockers.
Activation boundary: Use When and Do Not Use When define when SkillGuard applies and when it must stay inactive.
Do not use this skill for generic README writing, ordinary package publication, broad repository cleanup, dependency upgrades, application feature work, or release notes unless the work is.
Do not use this skill for unrelated coding tasks that do not involve a skill, skill repository, skill-like workflow, or skill-release boundary.
Do not use this skill merely because a repository contains Markdown, Python, tests, or a release process. The task must involve skill maintenance, skill activation, skill validation, or ski.
Do not use this skill to certify AI correctness, guarantee Codex activation, or bypass human review. SkillGuard can require evidence and structure judgment records, but it cannot prove that.
The target section 'Do Not Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Use the repository README, pyproject metadata, VERSION file, and AGENTS file for the public repository contract, metadata, version, and contributor boundaries only when the source repositor.
Use the repository references directory for the maintained SkillGuard standards only when the source repository layout is present.
Confirm the scope and target.
Identify the target skill path, repository root, maintained target, suite file, or release artifact. Record whether files already exist and preserve user or peer-agent work.
Inspect the current materials before editing or judging them.
Inventory the relevant files.
The target section 'Required Workflow' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
State whether the task is for one skill, a suite of skills, a public repository foundation, a release check, or a repair. If the target is unclear, block or ask for the missing information.
Treat local scripts/ and fixtures/ under this skill as evidence only after direct inspection in the current task finds those paths and their current content. Treat repository tests and exam.
Use the skillguard-global-router skill and the scan-global-skills, build-global-registry, check-global-registry, resolve-global-skill, render-global-prompt, install-global-prompt, check-glo.

workflow_stage_inventory.summary: advance phases only when required evidence and checks are present for earlier phases;
Apply judgment checks only after deterministic evidence is clear.
bind native routes and native checks directly when the target skill already owns routing, validation, controller, or closure behavior; do not create a second SkillGuard execution router bes.
compile or maintain a .skillguard/work-contract.json that records the integration mode before any runtime claim: native-integrated when the target skill already has its own route/check syst.
create or update a .skillguard/runs/ run record only when the contract is skillguard-runtime and SkillGuard owns the execution route;
Initialize or update maintained records only when the current task owns that work.
Inspect the current materials before editing or judging them.
require the native route decision or SkillGuard-owned route decision before non-trivial skill work starts, depending on the declared integration mode;
run route, phase-order, evidence, quality-floor, freshness, suite-child, and closure checks before closure;
Target workflow section: Runtime Contract Mode
